quoteFirst: If there was time enough before the arrival of the officer, troopers who had sufficient money could go and purchase at the canteen any small articles which were needed to complete their kit (sponges and chamois leathers were especially apt to be stolen from _Volontaires_, as it was impossible to identify such articles). At
quoteLast: we all had to be ready at the foot of our beds; and the Sergeant-major passed through the room to make sure that everything was in order, and every trooper properly attired. A quarter of an hour later the cry of "_Fixe!_" (Attention) was uttered by the trooper standing nearest to the door.
title: Trooper 3809: A Private Soldier of the Third Republic

quoteFirst: Then the nerve-racking wait commenced. Every now and then I would glance at the dial of my wrist-watch and was surprised to see how fast the minutes passed by. About
quoteLast: I got nervous waiting for our guns to open up. I could not take my eyes from my watch. I crouched against the parapet and strained my muscles in a death-like grip upon my rifle.

quoteFirst: They remained under this delusion until half past one o’clock in the afternoon, when, to their great surprise, Admiral Togo, with four battle-ships and eight armored cruisers, appeared directly ahead. … At
quoteLast: , when the flag-ships of the two fleets were a little more than four miles apart, Togo hoisted the following signal: ‘The fate of the Empire depends upon this battle. Let every man do his best.’ At two o’clock the Japanese squadrons on the flank and rear of the Russians closed in a little, and eight minutes later the fight began, Admiral Togo opening fire at a distance of about four miles.
title: History for ready reference, Volume 7

quoteFirst: “28.--Fired several shots at the chace. At
quoteLast: perceived her hoist two lights and bring to. At two shortened sail, hove to, boarded, and took possession of the chace, which proved to be the American schooner privateer _Swordfish_, out sixteen days from Boston, armed with twelve six-pounders and eighty-two men. During the chace ten of her guns and several spars were thrown overboard.
title: Jane Austen's sailor brothers
